title: Dullard needs help on Formulae
excerpt: Hi there, I’m wondering if what I want to do is possible. I have a status box, it can be changed to dropdown if necessary, I want to check the value of the status/dropdown and depending on such value I want the box to re…

posts.author: kstirzaker (Kevin Stirzaker)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there, I’m wondering if what I want to do is possible. I have a status box, it can be changed to dropdown if necessary, I want to check the value of the status/dropdown and depending on such value I want the box to reflect the value of one of a set of other box’s.

image2088×232 18.1 KB

So for instance, if the status/dropbox = defined, I would want another column to be populated with the contents of SP-CMM 2. NOTE: These are log text box’s.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@kstirzaker,

I would recommend using the SWITCH function. The basic format would be as follows:

SWITCH({Status}, "Defined", {SP-CMM 2. NOTE}, "Second Test Value", {2nd Result Column}, "Third Test Value", {3rd Result Column}, "Value Returned on No Matches")

You can specify as many “on this value”, “return this” pairs as you want.

posts.author: mchinsky (Mark Chinsky)
posts.cookedHtml: Wish they used the traditional Case syntax. Its alot more intuitive

posts.author: kstirzaker (Kevin Stirzaker)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you so much.

This works brilliantly. This old Podge is going to look so clever to his colleagues

All I need to do now is make all the text wrap in the cell as it does in a long text cell Or, am I asking the impossible

posts.author: kstirzaker (Kevin Stirzaker)
posts.cookedHtml:
Erm, I don’t even know what the means

Mind you, I did say I was a dullard

But, I do so love Monday Boards.

posts.cookedHtml: This topic was automatically closed 7 days after the last reply. New replies are no longer allowed.
